spec.description: Proj Clarion readiness gate: five signal rows (Reliability · Cost · Latency · Quality · Engagement) answering "can this leave dev?". Postgres for pipeline outcomes; Tempo/gen_ai for LLM telemetry.

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval),
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (finished_at-started_at)))::numeric, 0) AS "P50 (s)",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (finished_at-started_at)))::numeric, 0) AS "P95 (s)"
FROM pipeline_phases
WHERE phase IN ('research', 'plan')
  AND status = 'done'
  AND $__timeFilter(started_at)
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

spec.description:
Duration of the research + plan phases (the LLM-driven pipeline steps). P95 spikes indicate slow Claude responses or prompt complexity.

⚠️ Once gen_ai.request.duration is added to spans, this panel can switch to direct model-level latency.
spec.title: LLM phase duration · P50 / P95

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval),
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE phase = 'research') AS "Research",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE phase = 'plan') AS "Plan"
FROM pipeline_phases
WHERE phase IN ('research', 'plan')
  AND $__timeFilter(started_at)
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

spec.description:
Count of research and plan phase executions per day — the LLM-driven stages of every pipeline.

Research = web scrape + AI synthesis. Plan = multi-step LLM planner generating the knowledge graph.
spec.title: Daily LLM phase runs

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  REPLACE(REPLACE(prompt_template, 'clarion.planner.', 'planner.'), 'research.', 'research.') AS "Template",
  COUNT(*) AS "Calls",
  ROUND(SUM(cost_usd)::numeric, 4) AS "Cost (USD)"  
FROM llm_calls
WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at)
  AND ('$model' = 'All' OR model = '$model')
GROUP BY prompt_template
ORDER BY "Cost (USD)" DESC

spec.description: Cost and call volume per prompt template in the last 7 days — shows which agent steps are most expensive. research.extract_signals (haiku) is cheap; clarion.planner.* (opus) drives most spend.
spec.title: Cost by prompt template

spec.description: Placeholder — requires AWS CloudWatch and Azure Monitor datasource connections in Grafana Cloud
spec.title: ☁️ multi-cloud agent coverage

options.content:
## ☁️ Multi-Cloud Agent Coverage
**⏳ Datasource connections required in Grafana Cloud**

| Platform | Agent System | Status | Action needed |
|---|---|---|---|
| **AWS** | AgentCore / Bedrock | 🔌 Not connected | Add AWS CloudWatch datasource → Bedrock model invocation metrics |
| **Azure** | Copilot Studio / AI Foundry | 🔌 Not connected | Add Azure Monitor datasource → Application Insights traces |
| **3rd Party** | Claude Code / Anthropic SDK | ✅ **This dashboard** | Clarion pipeline is your live reference implementation |
| **On-prem** | Internal LLMs | 🔌 Not connected | Deploy Grafana Alloy → scrape local OTLP endpoint |

*Once connected, all platforms feed unified cost, latency, violation, and token metrics in a single view — the core consolidation story for migrating from AMG.*

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  $__timeGroupAlias(created_at, $__interval),
  SUM(input_tokens) AS "Input Tokens",
  SUM(output_tokens) AS "Output Tokens",
  SUM(cache_read_tokens) AS "Cache Read",
  SUM(cache_write_tokens) AS "Cache Write"
FROM llm_calls
WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at)
  AND ('$model' = 'All' OR model = '$model')
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

spec.description: Daily breakdown of input, output, and cache tokens. Cache read tokens cost ~90% less — growing cache read vs input ratio means caching is working. Output token growth signals longer agent responses.
spec.title: Token consumption trend

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  COALESCE(agent_name, '(untagged)') AS "Agent",
  COUNT(*) AS "Calls",
  ROUND(SUM(cost_usd)::numeric, 4) AS "Cost (USD)",
  ROUND(AVG(cost_usd)::numeric, 4) AS "Avg $/Call",
  SUM(input_tokens + output_tokens) AS "Total Tokens",
  COUNT(CASE WHEN error_type IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS "Errors"
FROM llm_calls
WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at)
  AND ('$model' = 'All' OR model = '$model')
GROUP BY agent_name
ORDER BY "Cost (USD)" DESC

spec.description: Per-agent breakdown of LLM calls, cost, and token consumption. Identifies which agents are most expensive and most active — key for regulated-environment cost attribution.
spec.title: Agent identity & cost summary

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(SUM(cost_usd)::numeric, 4) AS "Total Cost (USD)"
FROM llm_calls
WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
  AND ('$model' = 'All' OR model = '$model')

spec.description:
Total LLM API spend in the last 7 days across all calls (from llm_calls.cost_usd).

💡 Enable prompt caching (cache_control on system prompts) to reduce this — cache_read_tokens cost ~90% less than fresh input tokens.

To track TTFT, enable streaming=True on Claude calls — the instrumentation will populate ttft_ms automatically.
spec.title: LLM cost · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  100.0 * SUM(cache_read_tokens) / NULLIF(SUM(input_tokens + cache_read_tokens), 0),
  1
) AS "Cache Hit %"
FROM llm_calls
WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
  AND ('$model' = 'All' OR model = '$model')

spec.description:
Prompt cache hit rate = cache_read_tokens / (input_tokens + cache_read_tokens) from llm_calls.

🔴 Currently 0% — Anthropic prompt caching requires adding cache_control: {type: ephemeral} to system prompt blocks in API calls. Once enabled, repeated research/plan prompts will hit cache and cut input costs ~90%.

🟢 >30%  🟡 10–30%  🔴 <10%
spec.title: Cache hit rate · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT agent_name) AS "value"
FROM llm_calls
WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
  AND agent_name IS NOT NULL
  AND ('$model' = 'All' OR model = '$model')

spec.description: Count of distinct agent names that made LLM calls in the last 7 days. Each agent_name maps to a specific pipeline role (research_agent, plan_agent, etc.).
spec.title: Active agents · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(input_tokens + output_tokens) AS "value"
FROM llm_calls
WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
  AND ('$model' = 'All' OR model = '$model')

spec.description: Sum of all input + output tokens consumed by LLM calls in the last 7 days. Used for capacity planning and cost forecasting.
spec.title: Total tokens · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN error_type IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS "value"
FROM llm_calls
WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
  AND ('$model' = 'All' OR model = '$model')

spec.description: % of LLM calls that resulted in an error (non-null error_type). High error rate indicates model availability issues, rate limiting, or prompt failures.
spec.title: LLM call error rate · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(error_type, 'success') AS "Status", COUNT(*) AS "Count"
FROM llm_calls
WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at)
  AND ('$model' = 'All' OR model = '$model')
GROUP BY error_type
ORDER BY "Count" DESC

spec.description: Breakdown of call outcomes: success vs error types. Any non-success slice represents a failure mode to investigate — rate_limit, context_exceeded, timeout, etc.
spec.title: LLM call outcomes

spec.rawSql: SELECT DISTINCT ON (service_name) service_name AS "Service", status AS "Status", latency_ms AS "Latency (ms)", checked_at AS "Last Checked" FROM system_health ORDER BY service_name, checked_at DESC

spec.description: Live health status of all downstream services (Anthropic, Postgres, Grafana Cloud). Checks run every ~60s.
spec.title: 🌡️ system health

spec.rawSql: SELECT v.agent_name AS "Agent", v.violation_type AS "Violation Type", COUNT(DISTINCT v.violation_id) AS "Count", v.severity AS "Severity", COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN NOT v.resolved THEN v.violation_id END) AS "Unresolved" FROM agent_policy_violations v JOIN pipelines p ON p.pipeline_id = v.pipeline_id JOIN demo_plans dp ON dp.plan_id = p.plan_id JOIN plan_audit_log pal ON pal.plan_id = dp.plan_id WHERE pal.actor NOT IN ('planner', 'system') AND ('$actor' = 'All' OR pal.actor = '$actor') GROUP BY v.agent_name, v.violation_type, v.severity ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT v.violation_id) DESC

spec.description: Agent policy violations by type and severity. Actor filter: ik = real SE builds, smoke-test = CI test runs.
spec.title: 🛡️ policy violations & guardrails

spec.rawSql: SELECT checked_at AS time, latency_ms AS "Anthropic" FROM system_health WHERE service_name = 'anthropic' ORDER BY checked_at
SELECT checked_at AS time, latency_ms AS "Postgres" FROM system_health WHERE service_name = 'postgres' ORDER BY checked_at
SELECT checked_at AS time, latency_ms AS "Grafana Cloud" FROM system_health WHERE service_name = 'grafana_cloud' ORDER BY checked_at

spec.description: Live API response latency per service sampled every ~60s. Shows Anthropic, Postgres, and Grafana Cloud. TTFT will populate once streaming is enabled on Claude calls.
spec.title: ⚡ API response latency

options.content:
C
Clarion.
OVERVIEW · DEV-TO-PROD READINESS · v0.9.3
5 signal rows · Reliability · Cost · Latency · Quality · Engagement — answering "can this leave dev?"
schema v2beta1postgres YOUR_POSTGRES_DATASOURCE_UIDtempo grafanacloud-traces

C
Clarion. / ai & llm
CLAUDE TELEMETRY · gen_ai semantic conventions via Tempo
LLM cost · token consumption · cache efficiency · agent error rate — panels query live from Postgres & Tempo

C
Clarion. / pipelines
PIPELINE EXECUTION · 6 PHASES · research → plan → approve → generate → provision → kg-publish
Success rate · MTTR · phase duration · error classes — panels query live from Postgres

C
Clarion. / quality & adoption
DOES THE OUTPUT LAND · approve → demo → retain
Demo activation · profile reuse · claim accept rate · SE engagement — panels query live from Postgres

C
Clarion. Guardrails & health
SAFE-TO-PROD AUDIT · POLICY VIOLATIONS · DEPENDENCY UPTIME
What you'd need to convince a regulated buyer that an agentic AI workload is production-ready: scoped tools, anomaly detection, and live dependency health on top of the Grafana AI Obs app.

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN status='done' THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN status!='running' THEN 1 END),0),1) AS "value"
FROM pipelines
WHERE started_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'

spec.description:
Percentage of pipelines that finished with status='done' in the last 24 h.

🟢 ≥95%  🟡 80–95%  🔴 <80%

A value below 85% fires an alert. Failed = Claude subprocess crashed, FK violation, or kg-publish exit. Cancelled = run abandoned mid-flight.
spec.title: Success rate · 24 h

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.finished_at - f.finished_at)) / 60.0), 1) AS "value"
FROM pipeline_phases f
JOIN pipeline_phases d
  ON d.pipeline_id = f.pipeline_id
  AND d.phase = f.phase
  AND d.status = 'done'
  AND f.status = 'failed'
  AND d.finished_at > f.finished_at
WHERE $__timeFilter(f.finished_at)

spec.description:
Mean Time To Recovery: average minutes from a phase failure to the same phase completing successfully in a retried pipeline.

🟢 <10 min  🟡 10–30 min  🔴 >30 min

High MTTR often means Claude subprocess needs a retry back-off or the orchestrator is waiting on an external dependency.
spec.title: MTTR (min)

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  COUNT(CASE WHEN status='done' THEN 1 END) AS done,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN status='failed' THEN 1 END) AS failed,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN status='cancelled' THEN 1 END) AS cancelled
FROM pipelines
WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at)

spec.description:
Total runs attempted in the dashboard time window, broken down by terminal status.

A rising Cancelled count usually means users are re-running before the previous pipeline finishes — consider adding a UI guard.
spec.title: Run outcomes · window

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(AVG(done_phases),1) AS "Phases / Success"
FROM (
  SELECT p.pipeline_id, COUNT(pp.phase) AS done_phases
  FROM pipelines p
  JOIN pipeline_phases pp ON pp.pipeline_id=p.pipeline_id AND pp.status='done'
  WHERE p.status='done'
    AND p.started_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
  GROUP BY p.pipeline_id
) t

spec.description:
Proxy cost signal: average number of LLM-backed pipeline phases completed per successful build vs per failed build.

⚠️ Full $ cost requires gen_ai.usage.input_tokens + output_tokens in Tempo traces (instrument with Grafana AI Obs SDK to unlock).

A high phase count on failed builds means LLM spend is wasted on runs that never complete.
spec.title: Avg phases / success · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (finished_at - started_at)))::numeric, 0) AS p50_s,
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (finished_at - started_at)))::numeric, 0) AS p95_s
FROM pipelines
WHERE status='done'
  AND $__timeFilter(started_at)

spec.description:
p50 and p95 total pipeline wall-clock time from started_at to finished_at for completed runs.

🟢 p95 < 5 min  🟡 5–10 min  🔴 >10 min

Users are waiting for this. A p95 above 10 min means one slow phase is dragging the whole run.
spec.title: E2E pipeline duration

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  phase,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN status='done' AND started_at >= NOW()-INTERVAL '24 hours' THEN 1 END)
    / NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN status!='running' AND started_at >= NOW()-INTERVAL '24 hours' THEN 1 END),0),0) AS "24h",
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN status='done' AND started_at >= NOW()-INTERVAL '7 days' THEN 1 END)
    / NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN status!='running' AND started_at >= NOW()-INTERVAL '7 days' THEN 1 END),0),0) AS "7d",
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN status='done' THEN 1 END)
    / NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN status!='running' THEN 1 END),0),0) AS "30d"
FROM pipeline_phases
GROUP BY phase
ORDER BY CASE phase WHEN 'research' THEN 1 WHEN 'plan' THEN 2 WHEN 'approve' THEN 3 WHEN 'generate' THEN 4 WHEN 'provision' THEN 5 WHEN 'kg-publish' THEN 6 ELSE 7 END

spec.description:
How often does each pipeline phase succeed, across three time windows.

Any cell below 90% is highlighted amber/red — that phase is the weakest link and needs prompt or code hardening before prod.

Phases run in order: research → plan → approve → generate → provision → kg-publish.
spec.title: Phase success rate matrix (%)

spec.rawSql:
SELECT error_class, COUNT(*) AS count
FROM (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN error ILIKE '%kg-publish%' THEN 'kg-publish exit'
      WHEN error ILIKE '%plan subprocess%' THEN 'plan subprocess error'
      WHEN error ILIKE '%research subprocess%' THEN 'research subprocess error'
      WHEN error ILIKE '%ForeignKey%' OR error ILIKE '%foreign key%' THEN 'DB FK violation'
      WHEN error ILIKE '%orchestrator crashed%' THEN 'orchestrator crash'
      WHEN error ILIKE '%timeout%' THEN 'timeout'
      WHEN error ILIKE '%rate limit%' OR error ILIKE '%429%' THEN 'LLM rate limit'
      ELSE NULL
    END AS error_class
  FROM pipelines
  WHERE status IN ('failed','cancelled')
    AND $__timeFilter(started_at)
) t
WHERE error_class IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY error_class
ORDER BY count DESC
LIMIT 5

spec.description:
Top error messages from failed + cancelled pipelines (parsed from pipelines.error column).

The categories are: subprocess exit codes, orchestrator crashes, DB constraint violations, and process-level errors. Each bar represents a distinct failure class.

🔴 Any error class appearing >2× in 7 d needs a code fix before prod.
spec.title: Top 5 error classes

spec.rawSql:
SELECT phase,
  ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (finished_at-started_at)))::numeric,0) AS avg_s,
  ROUND(MAX(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (finished_at-started_at)))::numeric,0) AS max_s,
  COUNT(*) AS runs
FROM pipeline_phases
WHERE status='done'
  AND $__timeFilter(started_at)
GROUP BY phase
ORDER BY avg_s DESC NULLS LAST

spec.description:
p50/p95/avg wall-clock seconds for each pipeline phase (done runs only). The 'plan' phase dominates because Claude writes the full demo plan JSON here.

If provision p95 >> p50, Grafana Cloud API has intermittent slowness. Target: plan p95 < 8 min.
spec.title: Pipeline phase duration (avg/max seconds)

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(100.0*COUNT(CASE WHEN applied=false THEN 1 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS "value"
FROM profile_audit_log
WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'

spec.description:
Fraction of profile_audit_log entries where the agent returned a result but applied=false — meaning the agent ran but produced no useful change.

🔴 Alert threshold: >5% schema validation / applied=false rate.

High rate = a prompt template is generating outputs that fail downstream validation. This is the cheapest signal that a prompt needs sharpening before prod.
spec.title: Agent applied=false % · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(100.0*COUNT(DISTINCT ds.plan_id)/NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT dp.plan_id),0),1) AS "value"
FROM demo_plans dp
LEFT JOIN demo_sessions ds ON ds.plan_id=dp.plan_id
WHERE dp.review_state='approved_for_provision'
  AND dp.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'

spec.description:
% of approved plans that had at least one demo session started (DemoSessionCard activated).

🟢 >60%  🟡 30–60%  🔴 <30%

Low activation means SEs are approving plans but not presenting them to customers — either the content isn't compelling or the SE workflow is broken.
spec.title: Demo activation % · 30 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "value"
FROM (
  SELECT ds.plan_id, MIN(ds.started_at) AS first_start, MIN(ds.finished_at) AS first_end
  FROM demo_sessions ds
  WHERE ds.status IN ('stopped','expired')
  GROUP BY ds.plan_id
) s
JOIN plan_audit_log pal ON pal.plan_id=s.plan_id AND pal.action='cloud.provisioned'
WHERE s.first_end <= pal.created_at + INTERVAL '30 minutes'
  AND pal.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'

spec.description:
Count of provisioned plans whose first demo session stopped or expired within 30 minutes.

This is the 'immediate dissatisfaction' signal: the SE opened the demo but quickly closed it, implying the generated content didn't land well with the customer.

🔴 Any value >0 in a 7-day window warrants a content review of those specific plans.
spec.title: Early teardowns < 30 min · 30 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT p.pipeline_id)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT pal.actor),0), 2
) AS "value"
FROM pipelines p
CROSS JOIN (
  SELECT DISTINCT actor FROM plan_audit_log
  WHERE actor NOT IN ('planner','system')
    AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
) pal
WHERE p.status != 'running'
  AND p.started_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'

spec.description:
Average number of pipeline runs per distinct active SE per day across the selected window.

Target for healthy adoption: >1 build per active user per day once in wider staging.

A low ratio (<0.5) with high DAU means SEs are logging in but not generating — likely UX friction or a content quality issue.
spec.title: Builds per user · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(100.0*COUNT(CASE WHEN plan_count>1 THEN 1 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS "value"
FROM (
  SELECT source_profile_id, COUNT(*) AS plan_count
  FROM demo_plans
  WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
  GROUP BY source_profile_id
) t

spec.description:
% of company profiles that have been used to generate more than one demo plan.

High reuse = SEs are iterating on the same prospect over time (a good sign: the tool fits into deal cycles).
Low reuse = SEs use Clarion once per prospect and move on (possibly one-shot demo tool only).

Target: >20% reuse in steady state.
spec.title: Profile reuse % · 30 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN prompt LIKE 'accept claim:%' THEN 'accepted'
    WHEN prompt LIKE 'dismiss claim:%' THEN 'dismissed'
    ELSE 'other (research)'
  END AS verdict,
  COUNT(*) AS count
FROM profile_audit_log
WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at)
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY count DESC

spec.description:
Breakdown of all profile audit actions by claim verdict.

'accept claim:X' = agent synthesized a fact, SE accepted it.
'dismiss claim:X' = agent synthesized a fact, SE rejected it.
Other = free-text prompt / question (these are research mode, not claim scoring).

A high dismiss rate on a specific claim type = that agent prompt is over-hallucinating.
spec.title: Claim accept / dismiss

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  pal.actor,
  COUNT(DISTINCT p.pipeline_id) AS builds,
  ROUND(100.0*COUNT(CASE WHEN p.status='done' THEN 1 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN p.status!='running' THEN 1 END),0),0) AS success_pct,
  COUNT(DISTINCT ds.id) AS sessions
FROM plan_audit_log pal
LEFT JOIN demo_plans dp ON dp.plan_id=pal.plan_id
LEFT JOIN pipelines p ON p.plan_id=dp.plan_id
LEFT JOIN demo_sessions ds ON ds.plan_id=dp.plan_id
WHERE pal.actor NOT IN ('planner','system')
  AND $__timeFilter(pal.created_at)
GROUP BY pal.actor
ORDER BY builds DESC

spec.description:
Full engagement summary table: per-actor builds, success rate, and average session duration.

Identify power users and SEs who ran builds but never activated a session (potential onboarding drop-off).

'Sessions' = how many times this SE demoed a Clarion-built plan to a customer.
spec.title: SE engagement summary

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval),
  ROUND(100.0*COUNT(CASE WHEN status='done' THEN 1 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN status!='running' THEN 1 END),0),1) AS "Success Rate %",
  COUNT(CASE WHEN status!='running' THEN 1 END) AS "Total Runs"
FROM pipelines
WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at)
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

spec.description:
Daily pipeline success rate trend. Shows both success rate % (line) and total run volume (bars) so you can distinguish "low success" from "low volume" days.

Target: stay above the 95% green threshold line. A downward trend over 3+ days = systemic regression.
spec.title: Success rate trend

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  $__timeGroupAlias(created_at, $__interval),
  COUNT(DISTINCT actor) AS "Daily Active SEs",
  COUNT(DISTINCT plan_id) AS "Plans Created"
FROM plan_audit_log
WHERE actor NOT IN ('planner','system')
  AND $__timeFilter(created_at)
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

spec.description:
Daily unique active SE count over the selected time window.

Use this to spot onboarding ramps (upward slope) vs. drop-off (downward slope after a spike).

A D7 retention >40% signals healthy habitual use rather than a single demo scenario.
spec.title: Daily active SEs + plans

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN status='done' THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN status!='running' THEN 1 END),0),1) AS "value"
FROM pipelines
WHERE started_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'

spec.description:
Same as 24 h gate but over rolling 7 days — gives a noise-smoothed view.

🟢 ≥95%  🟡 80–95%  🔴 <80%

If 24 h dips but 7 d holds, it was likely a one-off blip. If both dip, treat as systemic.
spec.title: Success rate · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(100.0*COUNT(CASE WHEN status IN ('failed','cancelled') THEN 1 END)
  /NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN status!='running' THEN 1 END),0),1) AS "value"
FROM pipelines
WHERE started_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'

spec.description:
% of total completed runs (done+failed+cancelled) that ended in failure or cancellation.

🔴 Alert threshold: >15% waste.

This is your LLM spend efficiency signal — every failed run burns tokens that produced nothing. Target <10%.
spec.title: Build waste % · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (finished_at-started_at)))::numeric, 0) AS p95_s
FROM pipeline_phases
WHERE phase = 'plan'
  AND status = 'done'
  AND started_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'

spec.description:
P95 wall-clock time for the 'plan' phase — the most LLM-intensive pipeline step. Once streaming is enabled, this will switch to actual token delivery latency.

🟢 <5 min  🟡 5–8 min  🔴 >8 min
spec.title: LLM plan phase P95 · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT actor) AS "value"
FROM plan_audit_log
WHERE actor NOT IN ('planner','system')
  AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'

spec.description:
Count of unique actors (SEs) who logged at least one plan_audit_log action per day.

This is the primary adoption signal. If DAU is flat or falling, SEs are not building demos with Clarion regularly.

Target: at least 2 distinct active SEs per day once in staging.
spec.title: Active SEs · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(SUM(cost_usd)::numeric, 2) AS "Cost"
FROM llm_calls
WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
  AND ('$model' = 'All' OR model = '$model')

spec.description:
Total LLM API spend tracked in llm_calls.cost_usd. Dominated by claude-opus-4-7 plan phase ($0.27/call avg).

💡 Enable Anthropic prompt caching to reduce by 40–60%.
spec.title: LLM cost · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN passed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS "Pass Rate"
FROM llm_evals
WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'

spec.description:
% of all llm_evals checks that passed in the last 7 days. Currently 9/10 checks pass — profile_json_complete fails (score=3, missing required fields).

🟢 ≥95%  🟡 80–95%  🔴 <80%
spec.title: Agent eval pass rate · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT model || ' · ' || phase AS "Model · Phase",
  ROUND(SUM(cost_usd)::numeric, 4) AS "Cost (USD)"
FROM llm_calls
WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
  AND ('$model' = 'All' OR model = '$model')
GROUP BY model, phase
ORDER BY "Cost (USD)" DESC

spec.description: Where your LLM spend is going. claude-opus-4-7 plan phase dominates. Use this to decide where caching or a cheaper model would have the most impact.
spec.title: LLM cost by model & phase · 7 d

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  eval_name AS "Check",
  SUM(CASE WHEN passed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::int AS "Passed",
  SUM(CASE WHEN NOT passed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::int AS "Failed"
FROM llm_evals
WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
GROUP BY eval_name
ORDER BY "Failed" DESC, "Check"

spec.description:
Pass/fail count per eval check. profile_json_complete is currently failing — the research agent is not populating all required profile fields.

Any red bar = a specific agent behavior to fix.
spec.title: Agent eval results by check · 7 d
Clarion · Dev-to-Prod KPIs

spec.rawSql: SELECT DISTINCT model FROM llm_calls WHERE model IS NOT NULL ORDER BY model
